title: Polymath flows-lite workflow
description: Workflow YAML schema for polymath-flows v0.1. Serial step replacement / insertion only. Unsupported keys must fail closed.

description.description: One-sentence summary of what the workflow does.

extends.description: Parent workflow reference in the form '<plugin>:<workflow>@<version>'. v0.1 supports serial step replacement and named-anchor insertion only.

requires.description: Hard dependencies. Workflow validation fails if any required plugin is not installed, or if a required capability has no provider configured in `.polymath/capabilities.yaml`. Either or both of `plugins` and `capabilities` may be declared; capabilities are the recommended shape for cross-provider work (issue tracker, observability, pager, etc.) and capability resolution adds the resolved adapter plugins to the effective plugin set transparently.

capabilities.description: Map of capability name (from shared/schemas/capabilities.schema.json) to either `true` (any provider OK) or a constraint object pinning a specific provider. The capability vocabulary is closed; declaring an unknown capability fails validation. Resolution looks up the configured provider in the project's `.polymath/capabilities.yaml`.

inputs.description: User-supplied inputs that the workflow expects at start time.

guards.description: Preconditions checked before the first step. Failing guard pauses workflow before any work.

steps.description: Ordered serial steps. v0.1: each step runs after the prior step completes successfully.

invoke.description: Routing label of the form '<plugin>:<capability>'. v0.1 is a label, not a programmatic slash-command call. May contain `${capabilities.<cap>.plugin}` or `${capabilities.<cap>.provider}` placeholders so workflows can be provider-agnostic; placeholders are resolved at run time from the project's `.polymath/capabilities.yaml`.

prompt.description: Instruction the executor presents when reaching this step. May reference ${inputs.*} and ${workflow.*} placeholders.

needs.description: Step IDs that must be complete before this step. v0.1 expects a single predecessor (implicit serial order).

artifacts.description: Files this step is expected to produce or update.

topology.description: Composition pattern for this step (§ 4.6). 'series' executes after the previous step (default if absent). 'fanout' is accepted by validation but executor still runs serially in v0.1.5 — actual parallel execution lands when the runtime supports it. Other patterns reserved.

override.description: When 'extends' is set, replace steps in the parent with the same id.

insertAfter.description: When 'extends' is set, insert additional steps after a named anchor step id in the parent.

mustPass.description: Deterministic checks the executor runs after the last step. Failure pauses the workflow with a violation summary.

severity.description: advisory checks run and report but cannot fail the workflow on their own. blocking checks halt the workflow on failure. If omitted, defaults are: stepSummaryMatches→advisory; everything else→blocking.

artifact.description: Artifact name (matches shared/schemas/artifacts/<name>.schema.json) for artifactValid / artifactSchemaStrict checks.

minBodyChars.description: For artifactSchemaStrict: minimum non-whitespace, non-heading characters in the body (after frontmatter). Catches hollow stub artifacts (e.g. '# PRD\n').

rejectAdditionalProperties.description: For artifactSchemaStrict: if true, reject any frontmatter field not declared in the artifact schema, regardless of the schema's own additionalProperties setting. Default true.

filesChanged.description: For diffConstraint: bound on number of files changed in the working tree (or since the given ref).

linesChanged.description: For diffConstraint: bound on total added+removed lines in the working tree (or since the given ref).

pathAllowlist.description: For diffConstraint: glob patterns; any changed path that does not match at least one allowlist entry fails the check.

pathBlocklist.description: For diffConstraint: glob patterns; any changed path that matches a blocklist entry fails the check.

since.description: For diffConstraint: git ref to diff against. Defaults to the working tree vs HEAD.
